summary: Crystal‑clear recording delivers an iconic ’84 set with epic Touch, a soaring Terrapin Station, and a breathtaking Morning Dew.
review: Both reviewers rave about the flawless sound—calling it “great sounding” and “exactly perfect”—and praise every song, especially the fake‑out ending to Touch, the laser‑rifﬁng Terrapin Station, and an unforgettable Morning Dew. Jerry’s guitar work is singled out as incandescent, Brent’s keyboards are praised, and Bob Weir’s rhythmic drive adds extra fire, making this a can’t‑miss show for any ’80s Dead fan.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviews are 5‑star and consistently laud the musical performance and audio fidelity; the weight of a stellar show (70%) plus excellent recording quality (30%) yields an near‑perfect rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the sound as crisp, perfect and “great sounding” with no reported glitches or dropouts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is described as an all‑around can’t‑miss performance with seamless energy, highlighted by a fake‑out Touch ending and an epic Terrapin Station that keeps the momentum soaring.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His solo tone, distortion and laser‑rifﬁng fret work are repeatedly praised as “epic” and “on fire.”
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for laying down a train/march cadence that fuels the jam, adding extra drive to the performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s vibe is highlighted as a key element of the Touch jam.
